rulings.Case Title: CALDERA PHARMECEUTICALS, INC., A DELAWARE VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON TUESDAY, MAY 20, 2008 LINE 5. DEFENDANTS THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AND LOS ALAMOS NATIONAL SECURITY, L.L.C.'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT: The demurrer of LANS to the second cause of action is sustained. Hearing required to determine if leave to amend will be granted. The amended complaint alleges that patent application '376 had not been timely filed prior to the execution of the License Agreement at issue (paragraphs 29-30) and that plaintiff was not aware of the fact that patent application '901 had been abandoned prior to the execution of the License Agreement (paragraphs 13 and 32). Plaintiff alleges that it incurred substantial costs based on the belief that these patents were viable and defendants failed to disclose the fact that the rights to '376 and '901 had not been perfected. From the face of the complaint, the wrongs about which plaintiff complains occurred prior to the assignment to LANS on April 25, 2006 and plaintiff had already sustained an injury as a consequence. Based on these facts, LANS, as assignee of a public entity, is entitled to immunity for the conduct of the Regents in abandoning '901 and failing to make the requisite filings as to the '376 as well as the alleged breach of duty on the part of the Regents in not disclosing these facts. Merely because LANS became an assignee does not give rise to a duty distinct from the duty to disclose to plaintiff that the Regents already allegedly breached. =(302/PJM)
